content: agent1: implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py (StreamListener class). Will add idle_timeout_s param, self._last_chunk_ts tracking, and tick() method. Let me know what file/methods you're touching (feature1 - is_last_chunk?) so we don't clobber each other.

agent1: ack. My tick()/idle_timeout_s work is done already: I appended idle_timeout_s as the LAST __init__ param (after allow_reuse) and added self._last_chunk_ts. If you're adding debug/debug_logger after allow_reuse, please add them BEFORE idle_timeout_s so idle_timeout_s stays last, or just re-order however you like as long as idle_timeout_s ends up last in the signature — I'll adjust after you land yours, just ping me. In receive() I only added one line right after the try/except chunk_message extraction: 'if chunk_message: self._last_chunk_ts = time.time()' — separate from your reset-block/start-identifier/rolling-end/return insertion points, should not overlap. I also added a brand new tick() method right after receive() (before flush()) — self-contained, shouldn't touch your flush() refactor. Let me know when your patch lands so I can verify no clobbering.

agent1: my idle_timeout_s/tick() implementation is complete and I've saved my patch.txt snapshot. Summary: __init__ gained idle_timeout_s (currently last param — happy to reorder if you land debug params after allow_reuse, just let me know the final order you want), receive() gained one line after the try/except chunk extraction to refresh self._last_chunk_ts, and a new tick(now_ts=None) method sits between receive() and flush(). tick() checks idle_timeout_s elapsed, emits StreamResponse(..., chunk='') (setting is_last_chunk=True via dataclasses.fields introspection if that field exists on StreamResponse — so it'll pick up your/feature1's field automatically whenever it lands), then unconditionally resets stream_start/stream_end/cache_hit/queues/_last_chunk_ts regardless of allow_reuse. Go ahead and land your debug logging changes — my edits are in different lines than what you described so should merge cleanly.
